pi_items.source: Task specification in task.json and setup_task.sh
Task specification in task.json and setup_task.sh

Verified against medical imaging literature and InVesalius documentation. Cortical bone ranges from 500-1000 HU; 350 HU is within reasonable range for broader bone segmentation including trabecular bone. Compared to InVesalius standard presets: Bone (226-3021), Compact Bone Adult (662-1988). 350 is lower than pure cortical bone but reasonable for this task's scope.

Verified against InVesalius presets and medical imaging standards. Value 2000 HU closely matches Compact Bone (Adult) preset upper limit of 1988 HU, and falls within standard bone range (226-3021 HU) documented in InVesalius v3.0 manual. Reasonable for cortical/compact bone segmentation.
Typical tolerance for CT Hounsfield unit segmentation in medical imaging

Reasonable minimum for cortical bone 3D models. Research shows bone models range from tens of thousands to millions of triangles depending on quality. 5000 is conservative minimum for meaningful 3D geometry.

Reasonable maximum for high-detail medical bone models. Literature indicates cortical bone models can have high polygon counts up to 2.5M depending on quality settings. 400K represents high-quality but manageable output.

Binary STL format: 84 bytes header + (50 bytes * triangle_count). 102400 bytes ≈ 1998 triangles minimum. Calculation: (102400 - 84) / 50 = 1998 triangles. This is consistent with the verifier.py file checking for stl_size_bytes > 100000.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the CT Cranium dataset from the official InVesalius v3.0 release (GitHub asset 0051.zip), which is real medical imaging data (not synthetic). The custom threshold parameters are scientifically sound: minimum 350 HU and maximum 2000 HU target a cortical/compact bone range, compared to the standard InVesalius Bone preset of 226-3021 HU. These thresholds are reasonable for bone segmentation and align with medical imaging literature where cortical bone ranges from 500-1000 HU. The triangle count ranges (5000-400000) and minimum STL file size (102400 bytes ≈ 2000 triangles) are consistent with typical medical 3D model specifications. All metadata values have been independently verified against official documentation, medical imaging standards, and calculated specifications.
